{"parts":[{"tool_name":"run_parallel_cmds","content":{"stdout":"[Command: cat /home/workspace/N5/builds/pulse-self-upgrade/deposits/D1.1.json]
{
  "drop_id": "D1.1",
  "status": "complete",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-07T19:45:00-05:00",
  "summary": "Successfully implemented Forward Broadcast functionality in Pulse orchestrator. Added broadcast collection and injection logic to enable forward-only communication between Drops.",
  "broadcast": "Forward Broadcast feature complete — deposits can now include 'broadcast' field which gets injected into subsequent Drop briefs",
  "artifacts": [
    "Skills/pulse/scripts/pulse.py"
  ],
  "decisions": [
    "Placed broadcast injection logic in spawn_drop() to ensure all new Drops receive broadcasts",
    "Store broadcasts in meta.json under 'broadcasts' key for persistence across orchestrator restarts",
    "Inject broadcasts before common section markers (Requirements, Context, Files to Read) or append at end",
    "Made broadcast field optional for backward compatibility"
  ],
  "concerns": [],
  "notes_for_orchestrator": "The implementation is fully backward compatible. Deposits without 'broadcast' field continue to work normally. The feature only activates when deposits include the optional 'broadcast' field. The next Drop (D1.2) should test this implementation."
}
[Command: cat /home/workspace/N5/builds/pulse-self-upgrade/deposits/D2.1.json]
{
  "drop_id": "D2.1",
  "status": "complete",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-08T00:50:00.000000+00:00",
  "summary": "Successfully implemented hypothesis racing pattern with first_wins mode. Added check_first_wins() function, superseded status handling, and updated STATUS.md display.",
  "broadcast": "Hypothesis Racing complete — use 'first_wins: true' in meta.json and 'verdict: confirmed' in deposits to race hypotheses",
  "artifacts": [
    "Skills/pulse/scripts/pulse.py"
  ],
  "decisions": [
    "Used 'verdict' field in deposits to determine hypothesis confirmation",
    "Default hypothesis_group to all Drops if not specified (most flexible)",
    "Only supersede pending/running Drops (not already complete/failed/dead)",
    "Added 'superseded_at' timestamp for tracking when superseding occurred",
    "Used [~] symbol in STATUS.md to denote superseded Drops for visual distinction"
  ],
  "concerns": [],
  "notes_for_orchestrator": "D1.2 (Integration Tests) should test the first-wins functionality. D2.2 (SKILL.md Update) should document the new first_wins feature including usage examples and the verdict field requirement.",
  "learnings": [
    "First-wins pattern is useful for debugging scenarios where multiple parallel theories need testing",
    "Superseded Drops should be clearly distinguished from failed/dead Drops since they were actively canceled",
    "The verdict field provides a clean way for Drops to signal hypothesis confirmation vs rejection"
  ],
  "errors": []
}
[Command: cat /home/workspace/N5/builds/pulse-self-upgrade/deposits/D3.1.json]
{
  "drop_id": "D3.1",
  "status": "complete",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-07T19:50:00-05:00",
  "summary": "Documented delegate-only mode as a prompt discipline for Pulse orchestrators. Created comprehensive reference documentation explaining when and how to use the discipline, what constraints apply, and how it maintains provenance clarity in multi-Drop builds.",
  "broadcast": "Delegate-only mode documented — use 'delegate_only: true' in meta.json to signal orchestrator should coordinate only, not edit code",
  "artifacts": [
    "Skills/pulse/references/delegate-only-mode.md"
  ],
  "decisions": [],
  "concerns": [],
  "notes_for_orchestrator": "D2.2 should add a brief summary to SKILL.md pointing to this reference doc"
}
[Command: cat /home/workspace/N5/builds/pulse-self-upgrade/deposits/D4.1.json]
{
  "drop_id": "D4.1",
  "status": "complete",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-07T19:55:00.000Z",
  "summary": "Implemented dynamic task claiming system for Pulse builds. Added task pool functionality with atomic claiming, status tracking, and pool worker support.",
  "broadcast": "Dynamic Task Claiming complete — use 'task_pool' in meta.json for worker Drops to self-assign from a shared task queue",
  "artifacts": [
    "Skills/pulse/scripts/pulse.py"
  ],
  "decisions": [
    "Used fcntl file locking for atomic claiming to prevent race conditions",
    "Injected pool instructions directly into worker briefs rather than requiring separate template files",
    "Added pool status display to STATUS.md for visibility",
    "Made task pool completely optional and backward compatible",
    "Implemented claim timeout via task status tracking (claimed_at timestamps)"
  ],
  "concerns": [],
  "notes_for_orchestrator": "Task pool system is fully implemented and tested. No breaking changes to existing functionality. Pool workers will automatically receive claiming instructions when spawned. D1.2 (tests) and D2.2 (documentation) can reference the new pool functionality.",
  "learnings": [
    "File locking with fcntl provides simple, reliable atomicity for concurrent task claiming",
    "Injecting instructions into briefs is more flexible than requiring template files",
    "Pool exhaustion handling is critical for graceful worker termination"
  ],
  "errors": []
}","stderr":"","returncode":0},"tool_call_id":"toolu_01L41PmfyBwzjFCTUxfcQKFZ","metadata":null,"timestamp":"2026-02-09T01:07:28.288498Z","part_kind":"tool-return"}],"timestamp":null,"instructions":"","kind":"request","run_id":null,"metadata":null}
